gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Iterative destruction (RFC-0041 §4.5's doc-IR member of the iterative-destruction class)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The **derived** recursive `Drop` this replaces walks the `children` spine one host-stack frame"] # [doc = " per depth level, so a deep tree (confirmed empirically down to n=50,000 — `guard_hole_census.rs`,"] # [doc = " `src/tests/ir.rs::walk_does_not_overflow_on_a_deep_chain`) overflows the stack on drop, independent"] # [doc = " of (and previously un-closed by) the [`Node::walk`] host-stack guard. `mycelium-doc` is a tooling"] # [doc = " crate, not the frozen kernel/L1 core §4.5 otherwise tracks, so this lands as a normal fix (no"] # [doc = " within-freeze channel needed)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Mechanics: `mem::take` the drop target's `children` into an explicit worklist `Vec<Node>`, then"] # [doc = " drain it depth-first, at each step also `mem::take`-ing the popped node's own `children` onto the"] # [doc = " worklist *before* that node is allowed to drop. By the time a worklist node's implicit drop glue"] # [doc = " re-enters this `impl Drop` (recursively, once per node), its `children` is already empty — so the"] # [doc = " recursion never goes deeper than one level; the depth that used to live on the host stack now lives"] # [doc = " in the worklist `Vec` on the heap. **No observable change** (destruction-order-only): `Node` has no"] # [doc = " `Drop`-visible side effects (no external resources), only memory to reclaim, so reordering *which*"] # [doc = " sibling frees first is unobservable — only that every node frees exactly once, which this"] # [doc = " preserves. A worklist-`Vec` allocation during `drop` is acceptable here (unlike the OOM/unwind-"] # [doc = " critical kernel path in §4.5): `mycelium-doc` is a build-time tooling crate, not on the interpreter's"] # [doc = " panic/unwind hot path."] impl Drop for Node { fn drop (& mut self) { let mut worklist : Vec < Node > = std :: mem :: take (& mut self . children) ; while let Some (mut next) = worklist . pop () { worklist . extend (std :: mem :: take (& mut next . children)) ; } } }
gaps.reason: trait-impl of non-prelude trait `Drop` — no ambient trait definition and synthetic trait emission is refused (DN-34 §8.8 / DN-122 MVP); gapped the whole impl so the residual cannot file-poison myc-check (G2; express gap-close 2026-07-16)

gaps.snippet: impl Node { # [doc = " Build a node, computing its content address from its content + children (ADR-003). Provenance"] # [doc = " is **not** hashed (metadata, not identity) so a re-flowed source line keeps the deep link"] # [doc = " stable."] # [must_use] pub fn new (anchor : impl Into < String > , title : Option < String > , level : Option < Level > , provenance : Provenance , payload : Payload , children : Vec < Node > ,) -> Node { let anchor = anchor . into () ; let mut h = DocHasher :: new () ; h . tag (payload . tag ()) ; h . str (& anchor) ; h . opt_str (title . as_deref ()) ; h . tag (level . map_or (0 , Level :: tag)) ; match & payload { Payload :: Document { source_kind } => { h . str (source_kind . as_str ()) ; } Payload :: Section | Payload :: Index => { } Payload :: Prose { text } => { h . str (text) ; } Payload :: Example { lang , source , checked , } => { h . str (lang) . str (source) . u64 (u64 :: from (* checked)) ; } Payload :: Xref { target } => { h . str (& target . raw) . tag (target . resolution . tag ()) ; if let XrefResolution :: Internal { anchor } = & target . resolution { h . str (anchor) ; } if let XrefResolution :: Dead { reason } = & target . resolution { h . str (reason) ; } } Payload :: ApiItem { signature , summary } => { h . opt_str (signature . as_deref ()) . opt_str (summary . as_deref ()) ; } Payload :: Undocumented { what } => { h . str (what) ; } } h . u64 (children . len () as u64) ; for c in & children { h . child (& c . id) ; } Node { id : h . finish () , anchor , title , level , provenance , payload , children , } } # [doc = " Depth-first pre-order visit of this node and its descendants."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **RFC-0041 §4.7 guard-hole close (W1, RR-29).** The whole walk runs on"] # [doc = " [`mycelium_workstack::ensure_sufficient_stack`]'s grown worker stack (a 256 MiB"] # [doc = " lazily-committed thread), so a pathologically deep IR tree (thousands of nested sections)"] # [doc = " walks to completion instead of overflowing the caller's host stack — `walk` stays infallible"] # [doc = " (`()`); the fix is that it now never `SIGABRT`s. The budget passed is"] # [doc = " `mycelium_workstack::RecursionBudget::default()` — its depth/mem/step ceilings play no role in"] # [doc = " W1 (the guard body only grows the host stack; it does not charge against the budget), so this"] # [doc = " closes the host-stack hole only — it does not introduce a new refusal path, and behavior is"] # [doc = " otherwise unchanged (`Declared`: a real depth/work-step ceiling for doc-IR walks is future"] # [doc = " work, not introduced silently here)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The guard is applied **once**, at this public entry — the recursion itself runs through"] # [doc = " [`walk_inner`](Self::walk_inner), which does **not** re-guard per level (guarding every"] # [doc = " recursive step would spawn a worker thread per node: wrong and needlessly expensive)."] pub fn walk < 'a > (& 'a self , f : & mut (dyn FnMut (& 'a Node) + Send)) { let budget = mycelium_workstack :: RecursionBudget :: default () ; mycelium_workstack :: ensure_sufficient_stack (& budget , move | | self . walk_inner (f)) ; } # [doc = " The unguarded recursive body of [`walk`](Self::walk). Runs entirely on the worker stack the"] # [doc = " public entry already established."] fn walk_inner < 'a > (& 'a self , f : & mut dyn FnMut (& 'a Node)) { f (self) ; for c in & self . children { c . walk_inner (f) ; } } }
gaps.reason: no member of this impl block could be transpiled (3 sub-issue(s)): impl method `new` signature: unsupported Rust type form `impl Into < String >`; impl method `walk` signature: lifetime parameter `'a` has no grammar surface; impl method `walk_inner` signature: lifetime parameter `'a` has no grammar surface

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The whole projected corpus: top-level documents plus the navigable index over every node."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Serialize)] pub struct DocModel { # [doc = " The top-level [`Payload::Document`] nodes, in projection order."] pub documents : Vec < Node > , # [doc = " anchor → content address, over **every** node (the search/navigation index, §4.1 #2)."] pub anchors : BTreeMap < String , ContentHash > , }
gaps.reason: struct `DocModel` uses named fields and references a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: impl DocModel { # [doc = " Assemble a model from projected documents, building the anchor index. The builders allocate"] # [doc = " globally-unique anchors, so a collision would be an internal bug; if one ever slips through,"] # [doc = " the duplicate key collapses here and the **navigability lint catches it** (it errors when the"] # [doc = " anchor count is below the node count — §4.1 #2). The detection is at lint time, not here."] # [must_use] pub fn new (documents : Vec < Node >) -> DocModel { let mut anchors = BTreeMap :: new () ; for d in & documents { d . walk (& mut | n | { anchors . insert (n . anchor . clone () , n . id . clone ()) ; }) ; } DocModel { documents , anchors } } # [doc = " Every node across every document, depth-first (the order a reader meets them)."] # [must_use] pub fn all_nodes (& self) -> Vec < & Node > { let mut v = Vec :: new () ; for d in & self . documents { d . walk (& mut | n | v . push (n)) ; } v } # [doc = " The set of content addresses present in the model (used by the dual-projection-parity lint)."] # [must_use] pub fn id_set (& self) -> std :: collections :: BTreeSet < String > { self . all_nodes () . iter () . map (| n | n . id . as_str () . to_owned ()) . collect () } }
gaps.reason: no member of this impl block could be transpiled (3 sub-issue(s)): impl method `new` body: qualified/associated-function call `BTreeMap :: new` did not resolve to a known-emitted associated fn — Mycelium calls are bare identifiers, and the only sound surface form for `Type::method(...)` is the mangled `Type__method` name the declaration side already emits for a receiver-less inherent-impl associated fn (DN-34 §8.13/8.14), referenced only when that exact declaration is PROVEN present (same-file, or a resolved M-1084 cross-nodule sibling). A primitive/std associated fn with no emitted decl (e.g. `i128::try_from`), an unresolved type, or a `self`-receiving method (excluded by construction — it stays reachable only from its own bare call site) all gap here rather than fabricate a call (G2/VR-5, DN-133); impl method `id_set` signature: qualified type path `std :: collections :: BTreeSet < String >` — collapsing to its last segment would risk colliding with an unrelated same-named local type (e.g. `std::cmp::Ordering` vs a local `Ordering`); left an explicit gap rather than guessed (VR-5)
